kill_fact: No kill_fact found. Every load-bearing figure verifies against primary sources, and the debate's own verdict is already cautious (C / conviction 2 / SoP $165 below the $177 price). The bear case — quality front-run, ~1.8:1 risk/reward against, FCF badly lags earnings, single-factor AI-capex concentration (infra 55% of sales), and a contested #2/#3 liquid-cooling lane — survives the kill-check intact. The SoP fair value (~$165) is anchored on the FRESHEST data (the Q1-2026 FY guide-raise midpoint adj. EPS $4.50), so there is no stale-anchor defect.
corrections: (1) FCF multiple soft: the dossier cites P/FCF ~75x but Q1-2026 FCF of $53.8M annualizes to ~$215M, i.e. ~133x on the Q1 run-rate vs the $28.6B cap; the ~75x only holds if the unproven 90-95% FY conversion guide lands in H2 — so '~75x' is a forward/blended figure, not a trailing one, and the cash-quality risk is if anything UNDERSTATED, reinforcing (not weakening) the bear. (2) Boyd Thermal closed Mar 12, 2026 (deal value $9.5B, ~22.5x 2026E EBITDA, ~$1.7B Boyd revenue ~90% liquid-cooling) per Eaton's primary release — the competitive-encroachment fact is confirmed and materially real, directly into nVent's white-space lane. (3) Current price $177.02 (close Jun 18, 2026) and 36.7x fwd / 161.7M sh verify; the risk/reward 'slightly through fair value' conclusion stands.
evidence: nVent Q1-2026 Form 8-K press release (filed Apr 2026, SEC EDGAR CIK 0001720635): sales $1.24B +53% / +34% organic, adj EPS $1.09, FCF $53.8M, backlog $2.6B, FY guide RAISED to organic 21-23% and adj EPS $4.45-4.55 — every load-bearing figure corroborated. https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001720635/000162828026029098/q12026nvtpressrelease.htm

Eaton Corp 8-K / company release: completed $9.5B Boyd Thermal acquisition on Mar 12, 2026 (~22.5x 2026E EBITDA; Boyd ~$1.7B rev, ~90% liquid cooling) into Electrical Global, building grid-to-chip rival directly in nVent's liquid-cooling white space — moat-contest fact verified. https://www.eaton.com/us/en-us/company/news-insights/news-releases/2025/eaton-signs-agreement-to-acquire-boyd-thermal--expanding-solutio.html

Siemens press release (2026): Siemens + nVent joint reference architecture for 100-MW NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 liquid-cooled AI data centers; nVent confirmed in Google Project Deschutes 5.0 CDU (OCP spec) — both named design-in wins verified against primary sources. https://press.siemens.com/global/en/pressrelease/siemens-and-nvent-release-joint-reference-architecture-purpose-built-nvidia-ai-data

stockanalysis.com (Jun 18, 2026 close): NVT $177.02, market cap $28.63B, forward P/E 36.67, 161.72M shares; net leverage 1.5x / net cash ~-$1.48B per Q1-2026 10-Q — price, multiple, share count and balance-sheet facts confirmed. https://stockanalysis.com/stocks/nvt/
